title: 1990s America
description: Check your understanding of major concepts and events in 1990s America.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did the **North American Free Trade Agreement**, or **NAFTA**, accomplish?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: NAFTA did not create a single currency for the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

choices.content: It eliminated trade barriers between the United States, Canada, and Mexico.
It created a single currency for the United States, Canada, and Mexico.
It established that the United States, Canada, and Mexico were permitted to trade freely with other nations in the world, eliminating earlier restrictions that only allowed intra-continental trade.

assessment_items.question_content:
What was one cause of increasing **globalization** in the 1990s? 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Radio was invented in the late nineteenth century.

choices.content: The fall of the Soviet Union, which allowed former communist bloc countries to integrate into the global market
The invention of the radio, which allowed broadcasts of international news and music to cross national boundaries
The emergence of New York City as an international center of finance

assessment_items.question_content:
Elected at a young age to the governorship of Arkansas, Bill Clinton became a leader of the **New Democrats**, a wing of the Democratic Party that

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Soviet Union had fallen by the time that Bill Clinton ran for president.

choices.content: sought to decrease the size and scope of the federal government.
sought to revive the strong anti-communism that characterized the Democratic Party before 1968.
hoped to win the support of young voters by advocating far-left policies.

assessment_items.question_content:
What did President Clinton’s **Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1993** seek to do?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The act was not related to transportation infrastructure.

choices.content: Raise taxes for the wealthiest Americans, and expand the earned income tax credit for low-wage workers
Slash federal budgets for social programs in order to achieve a surplus
Repair transportation infrastructure in nine major cities

assessment_items.question_content:
What made the televised operations of **Desert Storm** distinct from the war footage that had emerged from Vietnam?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Television footage of the Vietnam War led to much more public protest than the coverage of Desert Storm, which was generally supported by the American people.

choices.content: Desert Storm was the first American conflict to be broadcast on live television.
There was no difference in the amount or quality of the footage, but Americans paid much more attention to the Vietnam War than they did to Desert Storm.
There was considerably more public protest for Desert Storm thanks to its broad media coverage.

assessment_items.question_content:
Why did the United States involve itself in the **Gulf War**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Gulf War took place from 1990-1991.

choices.content: Iraq had invaded the wealthy but small oil-producing nation Kuwait; the international community feared this was Iraq’s first step in conquering the entire Arab Peninsula.
Iraq had sheltered the terrorists responsible for the September 11, 2001 attacks on the World Trade Center and the Pentagon.
Iraq had fomented an OPEC oil embargo that threatened to undermine US energy security.

assessment_items.question_content:
What was significant about the **election of 2000**? 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: It is not possible to win the presidency without receiving more than half of the votes in the Electoral College.

choices.content: It was the first American election to be decided by the Supreme Court; the decision in _Bush v. Gore_ halted a recount of votes in the key state of Florida and resulted in George W. Bush winning the presidency
It was the first American election where the candidates received exactly the same number of popular votes, which called into question the validity of the Electoral College
It was the only American election in which a candidate won the presidency without winning a majority of the popular vote _or_ more than half of the votes in the Electoral College
